figures.caption: Figure 4: (a) Reasoning for the query in Figure 1 after identifying the candidate artist, Otto Knows from Sweden. (b) Average number of correct vs. incorrect claims (hypotheses) using k most recent reasonings. This is averaged across trajectories for the k = all setting (Section 5.3)

Figure 3: Effect of embedding k history turns. We fix the Agent to Tongyi-DR. Plot (a) shows the end-to-end accuracy of embedding past k turns, where “None” denotes the “AgentIR-4B w/o Reasoning” entry in Table 2. Plot (b) shows the ratio of unique clues covered by using k most recent reasonings, among all clues that have been covered. This is averaged across all trajectories for the k = all setting (Section 5.3).
Figure 12: A hypothetical document generated by Reason-Rewriter-7B for the query “backroom studio early 2010s euphoric”. 21

Figure 1: Reasoning-Aware Retrieval (AgentIR-4B) vs. conventional retrieval (Qwen3Embedding-4B) for a task from BrowseComp-Plus, paired with the Tongyi-DR agent. The task has been simplified for display.
Figure 14: Prompt used to extract the number of correct vs. incorrect claims (Step 2).
Figure 13: Prompt used to extract the ground truth multi-hop answer list (Step 1).

Table 3: Ablation over trajectory variants. All models are finetuned from Qwen3-Embedding4B using DR-Synth (Section 3.3), varying only the trajectory transformation f (Ht) used as the retriever’s input. “None” embeds only the query, corresponding to the AgentIR-4B (w/o Reasoning) entry in Table 2.
Figure 6: Prompt template used for the oracle reranker (Section 3.3).

Table 1: End-to-end evaluation on BrowseComp-Plus. For Tongyi-DR with the visit tool, Search Calls are reported as (search + visit). LLM-Rerank refers to listwise reranking the top-20 results with Qwen3-8B.
Figure 8: Prompt used for the “Prior Queries & Reasonings” ablation.
Figure 7: Prompt used for the “Prior Queries” ablation.

Figure 5: The prompt template used to embed for AgentIR-4B. At turn t, we fill in {reasoning} with τt and {query} with qt. Note that the duplicate “Query:” is intentional due to Qwen3-Embedding-4B Zhang et al. (2025)’s instruction format.

Table 2: Component ablation. All methods use Qwen3-Embed-4B as the backbone. “AgentIR-4B (w/o Training)” prepends reasoning traces without additional finetuning. “AgentIR-4B (w/o Reasoning)” trains on DR-Synth-generated WebShaper data (Section 3.3) but embeds only the query.
Figure 11: Prompt for assigning atomic clues to reasoning text.
Figure 9: Prompt used for the “Prior Queries & Reasonings & Docs” ablation.
Figure 10: Prompt for decomposing reasoning traces into atomic clues.
Figure 2: Oracle reranking procedure used in DR-Synth (Section 3.3)
